---
name: Spec Change Proposal
about: Propose a change to the Sploosh language specification
title: "[Spec] "
labels: spec, RFC, needs-design
assignees: ''
---

## Spec Section

Which section(s) of the [Language Spec](https://github.com/StreamDemon/sploosh/blob/main/docs/spec-plans/LANGUAGE_SPEC.md) does this change? (e.g., "Section 3.5 - Trait System")

## Current Behavior

What the spec currently says or implies.

## Proposed Change

What you want the spec to say instead.

## Syntax Example

```sploosh

```

## Rationale

Why this change improves the language. Consider the Sploosh design principles:

1. One way to do everything
2. Familiar vocabulary only
3. Explicit over implicit
4. Errors are values
5. Concurrency is structural
6. Dual-target by design
7. Spec fits in a prompt

## Impact Assessment

- **Breaking change:** Yes / No
- **Affects on-chain code:** Yes / No
- **Affects existing spec examples:** Yes / No
- **Estimated token budget impact:** (increases/decreases/neutral to the 4K token prompt budget)

## Prior Art

How do other languages handle this? (Rust, Elixir, Solidity, TypeScript, etc.)
